inline_comments.message: With VES/http removal (https://gerrit.onap.org/r/#/c/dcaegen2/platform/blueprints/+/104534/), VES-tls will use the default "dcae-ves-collector". The dns_name and service_name for VES-TLS input (in OOM) will be changed accordingly. This is needed to match the SAN defined in AAF

The healthcheck uses the name of the Kubernetes deployment, not the DNS name.  The deployment name is based on the first parameter of the call to the deploy function in the bootstrap script, and that has not changed.

Healthcheck queries Kubernetes about the status of a deployment.  Kubernetes checks the status of the deployment by directly accessing the container(s) associated with the component, by making an HTTP(S) request, by doing a simple TCP connection, or by running a script in the container, depending on how the probes are set up.

Sorry for the confusion.  The Kubernetes deployment name is based on the component name, so if you're changing the inputs to use the same component name that was used for the non-tls component, we should be looking for dep-dcae-ves-collector, not dep-dcae-ves-collector-tls.
